content: I'm feeling a bit frustrated with my work right now. I've been recovering from a knee injury and it's been hard to keep up with my responsibilities as a veterinarian.
I'm so sorry to hear that, Spencer. Recovering from an injury can be tough, both physically and mentally. How's your knee doing now?
It's getting better, but it's still a bit of a challenge. I've had to adjust my routine and find new ways of doing things that don't put too much strain on my knee.
I can imagine. It's great that you're being proactive and finding ways to adapt. Have you considered reaching out to your colleagues for support or help with your workload?
I have, and they've been really understanding and helpful. I've also been listening to a lot of podcasts to take my mind off things and learn something new.
I've been enjoying a true crime podcast and a podcast about animal behavior. It's really interesting to learn about the different ways that animals interact with each other and their environments.
That sounds fascinating. I'm sure it's helpful to have something to take your mind off your injury and work. Do you have any fun plans for when you're feeling better?
I do, actually. I'm planning a trip to visit my family in Colorado for Christmas. I'm really looking forward to seeing them and getting out into the mountains for some hiking and fresh air.
That sounds like a wonderful trip. I'm sure you'll have a great time and come back feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
I'm also thinking about starting a new hobby, maybe knitting or something like that. I've always been interested in it, but never had the time to try it out.
Knitting can be a great hobby, it's very relaxing and creative. You could even make some scarves or hats for your colleagues or family members as gifts.

That's a great idea, I'll have to look into it. And I'll also have to start thinking about my work projects again, maybe I can find some ways to incorporate my love of animals and the outdoors into my work.
I think that's a great idea, Spencer. It's always good to find ways to make your work more enjoyable and fulfilling.
